data.basisOfEstimate: No pricing estimate is possible because Horus Technology (Interesting Interests Inc.) is an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner specializing in intelligent document automation, cloud-native development, and AI/ML solutions. This solicitation requires a commercial cloud-based financial ERP software system (replacing QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024) with core modules including accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger, budgeting, project accounting, purchasing, and fixed assets. The company does not have an ERP software product to propose. No government estimated value was provided in the solicitation. Typical cloud ERP implementations for small public agencies (5-10 users) range from $100K-$500K for implementation plus $30K-$100K annually for SaaS subscriptions, but these are speculative market ranges and irrelevant since the company cannot perform this work. The evaluation weights pricing at only 20%, with software capabilities (Attachment A) at 30% and technical approach at 25% — areas where Horus Technology would score zero without an ERP product. Organization labor rates provided (Software Engineer $171.40/hr, Manufacturing Engineer $178.89/hr) are not applicable to this procurement which requires ERP software licensing and implementation by a qualified ERP vendor.
assumptions: Horus Technology does not possess or resell a commercial financial ERP software product
The company has no past performance implementing financial/accounting ERP systems for public agencies
This solicitation is seeking an established ERP vendor with a proven COTS product, not custom software development
The 5-10 user count and operational metrics suggest a small-to-mid-market ERP solution is appropriate
Competitive field will consist of established public-sector ERP vendors with mature products and extensive public agency references
No government estimated value was stated in the solicitation
The provided org labor rates are for custom development work and are not applicable to ERP software licensing/implementation pricing models
pricingRisks: Fundamental industry mismatch: Horus Technology is an AWS consulting/custom development firm, not a financial ERP software vendor — the solicitation requires a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) cloud-based accounting and financial reporting ERP system
The company does not possess a financial ERP product with modules for accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger, budgeting, project accounting, purchasing, or fixed assets
Competitors will be established ERP vendors (e.g., Tyler Technologies, Oracle NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Microsoft Dynamics, Workday) with proven public-sector financial software products
The 11-year maximum contract term requires a mature, supported software product with long-term viability — custom-built solutions carry extreme risk for this scope
Pricing must remain firm for 3 years with CPI-U/3% cap on escalation — typical for SaaS ERP vendors but unusual for custom development engagements
No past performance in financial ERP implementation for public agencies or grant-funded organizations as required in Section 4.2
Attachment A (Software Abilities and Requirements) likely requires demonstrating existing product capabilities that Horus Technology does not have
recommendedActions: NO-BID RECOMMENDED: This solicitation requires a commercial ERP software product for public-sector financial management — Horus Technology does not have such a product and cannot competitively respond
If pursuing despite mismatch, the only viable path would be as a subcontractor or integration partner to an established ERP vendor, providing cloud migration or data conversion services
Monitor for future IT modernization or custom software development opportunities from Sites Project Authority that align with AWS/cloud capabilities
Consider partnering with ERP vendors who may need AWS infrastructure or integration support for their implementations

evaluationFactors: Section 1: Response to Project Goals and Scope: 10%
Section 2: Proposed Strategy and Technical Approach: 25%
Section 3: Integration, Scalability, and Future Module Compatibility Narrative: 5%
Section 4: Firm Information, Qualifications, and References: 10%
Attachment A: Software Abilities and Requirements: 30%
data.overview: The Sites Project Authority (a California JPA) seeks proposals for acquisition and implementation of a cloud-based accounting and financial ERP software system to replace QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024. The solution must support core financial modules (AP, AR, GL, budgeting, project accounting, purchasing, fixed assets, bank reconciliation, cash management), with implementation targeting a January 1, 2027 go-live. The initial contract term is 1 year with up to two 5-year extensions (11 years maximum). Success means a streamlined, auditable, scalable financial system with dedicated support for a fast-paced infrastructure project transitioning from planning to construction and operations.

requirements.requirement: Provide an integrated cloud-based ERP solution covering Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Bank Reconciliation, Budget Management, Cash Management, Fixed Assets, General Ledger, Project Accounting, and Purchasing
Import prior one completed fiscal year of transactions, year-end balances, and current fiscal year-to-date transactions from QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024
Configure software to meet Sites Authority requirements and provide a Configuration Workbook documenting system settings
Provide a Chart of Accounts Mapping showing how ERP codes align with legacy codes
Provide a Testing and Acceptance Plan
Assist with financial report writing and development
Support 5-10 ERP users and 10-20 workflow approvers
Target go-live date of January 1, 2027
Solution must be modular, extensible, and capable of supporting project delivery through construction and operations
Provide ongoing cloud hosting services with automated updates and software change communications
Provide help desk support including remote support and on-call assistance, with on-site support available as needed
Provide implementation-phase support during cutover, go-live, and initial stabilization
Describe availability of documented APIs or other integration methods for third-party systems
Describe optional module capabilities for capital asset maintenance management, debt management, investment management, HR, payroll, timekeeping, and inventory tracking
Provide personalized training and manuals for power users
Provide at least one recorded training session and electronic training materials for all staff with role-appropriate training
Provide support for first 12 months post-implementation
Conduct weekly meetings for progress, budget, and timeline monitoring
Provide a personnel management plan with dedicated project manager and technical staff
Provide documentation demonstrating measures for protecting financial data in the SaaS environment and secure access controls (Attachment G)
All data entered into the ERP system shall remain sole property of the Sites Authority with full non-proprietary data exports upon request and upon contract termination at no additional cost
Pricing for software and services must remain firm for at least 3 years from contract execution with subsequent increases limited to annual CPI-U or 3%, whichever is less
Proposals must be offered for a term not less than 180 calendar days
Optional module pricing must remain eligible for up to 5 years from proposal submission date
Provide proof of insurance including workers compensation and commercial general liability
Provide minimum of three references for whom similar services have been performed
Disclose location of workers and identify any work performed outside the United States
Provide certification of financial condition signed by authorized representative
Identify key personnel anticipated to support implementation and ongoing services
Describe firm qualifications including experience implementing accounting/financial software for public agencies or grant-funded organizations

format: Electronic proposals in Adobe Acrobat or Microsoft Word format
Proposals shall not exceed 40 pages including transmittal letter and any appendices
Transmittal letter must not exceed two pages
Attachment B Pricing Proposal must be submitted as a separate file
Attachment A Software Abilities and Requirements Excel workbook submitted with proposal
attachmentsAndForms: Attachment A - Software Abilities and Requirements.xlsx
Attachment D - Location of Workers Utilized by Respondent
Attachment E - Certification of Financial Condition

deliverables: Project Management Plan with weekly meetings
Data conversion and migration from QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024
Personalized training and manuals for power users
At least one recorded training session and electronic training materials

redFlags.whyItMatters: HORUSTECH's available past performance references (Avue Technologies AI/ML project, Ford Credit automation) do not align with the core requirement of implementing cloud-based financial ERP software for a public agency. Attachment A (Software Abilities and Requirements) carries 30% evaluation weight and Section 4.2 specifically asks for 'Experience implementing accounting and financial software for public agencies or grant-funded organizations' at 10% weight — together representing 40% of the evaluation.
redFlags.mitigation: Seriously evaluate bid/no-bid decision. If proceeding, establish a formal teaming arrangement with an ERP software vendor that has strong public sector references and can serve as the primary software provider, with HORUSTECH in a supporting technology/integration role.
redFlags.flag: No demonstrated ERP software or public sector accounting experience

redFlags.whyItMatters: Attachment H requires the Respondent to 'Provide the Respondent's proposed contract agreement for software and services,' which is unusual — the Authority expects to use the vendor's standard software/services contract as a starting point. This implies the Authority expects respondents to be established software vendors with existing commercial agreements, not custom development firms.
redFlags.mitigation: If partnering with an ERP vendor, leverage that vendor's standard SaaS/services agreement as the base contract. Ensure it incorporates the Authority-specific provisions outlined in Attachment H (non-exclusivity, change-order controls, optional module add-on, pricing validity, scope assurance).
redFlags.flag: Respondent must submit its own proposed contract agreement

risks.whyItMatters: Section 4.2 requires 'Experience implementing accounting and financial software for public agencies or grant-funded organizations' (10% evaluation weight). HORUSTECH's past performance appears focused on AI/ML and document processing projects (e.g., Avue Technologies), with no evident ERP or accounting software implementation experience for public agencies.
risks.mitigation: Identify and partner with an ERP vendor or subcontractor that has demonstrated public agency financial software implementation experience. Consider teaming arrangements with firms that have California JPA or special district references.
risks.flag: Potential lack of public agency ERP implementation experience

risks.whyItMatters: The RFP requires proposing a cloud-based accounting and financial ERP software system with core modules (GL, AP, AR, Purchasing, Fixed Assets, etc.) per Section 5.1 and Attachment A. HORUSTECH would need to either be a software vendor or an authorized reseller/implementation partner for an established ERP product. No evidence of an existing ERP software product or vendor partnership was found.
risks.mitigation: Establish a formal partnership or reseller agreement with an established cloud ERP vendor (e.g., Tyler Technologies, Sage Intacct, Oracle NetSuite) that serves public sector clients before proposal submission.

risks.whyItMatters: Section 2.5 requires proposals remain valid for 180 calendar days and optional module pricing must be honored for up to 5 years from submission. Section 2.5 also requires firm pricing for 3 years post-execution with CPI-U or 3% cap on increases thereafter. This creates long-term pricing risk, especially if partnering with a third-party ERP vendor whose pricing may change.
risks.mitigation: Negotiate back-to-back pricing commitments with any ERP software partner before proposal submission. Build appropriate margin buffers into the pricing proposal to account for long-term cost escalation risk.
risks.flag: 180-day pricing validity and 5-year pricing eligibility commitment

risks.whyItMatters: Section 4.1 limits proposals to 40 pages including transmittal letter and appendices, while requiring a transmittal letter, four narrative sections, and eight attachments (A through H). This is a tight page budget that could constrain the ability to fully address all evaluation criteria.
risks.mitigation: Develop a detailed page allocation plan early. Prioritize Section 2 (25% weight) and Attachment A (30% weight) content. Use concise formatting and consider whether attachments in prescribed formats count toward the page limit or are separate files.
risks.flag: 40-page proposal limit with extensive required content

risks.whyItMatters: Per Section 2.2, finalists may be required to participate in software demonstrations and interviews between April 27 - May 15, 2026. The solicitation states the Authority 'may select a finalist group of Respondents to take part in an interview and software demonstration.' This requires having a demonstrable ERP product ready to show.
risks.mitigation: Ensure access to a demo environment of the proposed ERP solution well before the finalist notification date of April 22, 2026. Prepare key personnel for live demonstration and Q&A sessions.
risks.flag: Software demonstration and interview phase for finalists

risks.whyItMatters: Section 6.1 Task 2 requires importing 'prior one (1) completed fiscal year of transactions, year-end balances, and current fiscal year-to-date transactions, from QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024.' Respondents must demonstrate capability and experience with QuickBooks data migration.
risks.mitigation: Document a proven data migration methodology from QuickBooks Desktop to the proposed ERP platform. Include specific tools or connectors used and reference similar migration projects.
risks.flag: Data migration from QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024

incumbentInfo.notes: The solicitation states the Sites Authority currently uses 'QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024 accounting software from Intuit' (Section 1.1). Intuit/QuickBooks is the current software product, not an incumbent contractor providing services. This is a new procurement for a cloud-based ERP system to replace desktop accounting software, not a recompete of an existing services contract.

data.confidenceExplanation: Confidence is 55 due to: no past performance data available (-20), fundamental industry mismatch between company capabilities and solicitation requirements (-15), and expired deadline (-10). The analysis is high-confidence in its NO-GO conclusion despite low overall confidence score — the mismatch is unambiguous.
requiredActions: NO-BID: Do not pursue this opportunity — fundamental capability and product mismatch
Monitor Sites Project Authority for future IT modernization or custom software development opportunities that align with AWS/cloud capabilities
Consider partnering with ERP vendors who may need AWS infrastructure or data migration support for their implementations as a subcontractor role in future similar opportunities

criteria.name: TECHNICAL_FIT
criteria.rationale: Fundamental industry mismatch. Horus Technology is an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner specializing in intelligent document automation, AI/ML, and cloud-native development. The solicitation requires a commercial off-the-shelf cloud-based financial ERP software product with modules for AP, AR, GL, budgeting, project accounting, purchasing, fixed assets, and cash management. The company does not possess an ERP software product, has no ERP implementation capabilities documented in its KB, and has no partnership with an ERP vendor. Attachment A (Software Abilities and Requirements) carries 30% evaluation weight and requires demonstrating existing product capabilities that Horus Technology does not have.
gaps: No commercial ERP software product to propose
No financial/accounting software implementation capability
No QuickBooks data migration experience
No ERP vendor partnership or reseller agreement
Cannot provide software demonstration required for finalist phase
No help desk or ongoing SaaS hosting capability for financial software
No chart of accounts mapping or financial report writing expertise

criteria.name: PAST_PERFORMANCE_RELEVANCE
criteria.rationale: No past performance data available. The company has zero relevant past performance projects in the database for ERP or financial software implementation. The KB shows past performance in AI/ML document processing (Avue Technologies) and automation projects, which are in a completely different domain. Section 4.2 explicitly requires 'Experience implementing accounting and financial software for public agencies or grant-funded organizations' — the company has none.
gaps: No ERP implementation past performance
No public agency financial software references
No accounting software implementation experience of any kind
Cannot provide the required minimum three references for similar services
No QuickBooks migration references

criteria.name: PRICING_POSITION
criteria.rationale: The pricing analysis confirms the company cannot competitively price this opportunity. Horus Technology does not have an ERP software product to license, making it impossible to provide the required software licensing, SaaS subscription, and implementation pricing. The pricing module explicitly states 'NO-BID RECOMMENDED' and assigns a price confidence of 10. Competitors will be established ERP vendors (Tyler Technologies, Sage Intacct, Oracle NetSuite) with mature pricing models for public sector clients. The company's labor rates for custom development are irrelevant to this procurement's pricing structure.
gaps: No ERP software product to price
No SaaS subscription pricing model
Cannot commit to 3-year firm pricing or 5-year optional module pricing without a product
Labor rates not applicable to ERP licensing/implementation pricing models

criteria.name: STRATEGIC_ALIGNMENT
criteria.rationale: This opportunity is for a commercial ERP software vendor serving public sector accounting needs — a completely different industry and business model from Horus Technology's AWS consulting, AI/ML, and document automation focus. No documented strategic plan or growth targets indicate expansion into ERP software or public sector financial systems. Pursuing this would be a distraction from core competencies with near-zero probability of success.
gaps: Completely different industry (financial ERP vs AI/ML document automation)
Different business model required (SaaS product vendor vs consulting)
No strategic documentation supporting expansion into ERP market
Would require building entirely new capabilities outside core competency

criteria.name: INCUMBENT_RISK
criteria.rationale: This is a new procurement, not a recompete. The current system is QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024 from Intuit, which is a software product not a services contract. There is no incumbent contractor. The playing field is level among ERP vendors responding. However, this advantage is irrelevant given the fundamental capability mismatch.
gaps: Established ERP vendors with existing public sector client bases will have natural advantages in references and product maturity

data.summaryJustification: CRITICAL NO-GO: Horus Technology is an AWS consulting firm specializing in AI/ML and document automation. This solicitation requires a commercial cloud-based financial ERP software product vendor with public agency implementation experience. The company has no ERP product, no financial software capabilities, no relevant past performance, and cannot price this work. The pricing analysis explicitly recommends NO-BID with 10% confidence. The submission deadline of April 1, 2026 has already passed (today is April 19, 2026), making this opportunity expired regardless of capability fit.
blockers: Submission deadline has passed (April 1, 2026 — 18 days ago)
Company does not possess a commercial financial ERP software product
No past performance in ERP or financial software implementation for public agencies
No ERP vendor partnership or reseller agreement in place
Cannot complete Attachment A (Software Abilities and Requirements) without an ERP product
Cannot provide software demonstration required for finalist evaluation
data.decisionRationale: NO_GO decision based on multiple critical disqualifiers: (1) Fundamental industry mismatch — company is an AI/ML consulting firm, not an ERP software vendor; (2) No ERP software product exists to propose — Attachment A (30% weight) requires demonstrating existing product capabilities; (3) No past performance in financial software implementation for public agencies (10% weight); (4) Pricing analysis explicitly recommends NO-BID with 10% confidence; (5) Submission deadline of April 1, 2026 has already passed — today is April 19, 2026; (6) Cannot provide required software demonstration for finalist phase. Composite score of 1.35 reflects critical gaps across all dimensions except incumbent risk.

confidenceDrivers.factor: No past performance data available for ERP or financial software
Fundamental industry mismatch — AI/ML consulting vs ERP software vendor
Pricing analysis explicitly recommends NO-BID with 10% confidence
No incumbent contractor — level playing field (irrelevant given other factors)

data.summary: The Sites Project Authority is soliciting proposals for the acquisition and implementation of a cloud-based accounting and financial reporting ERP system to replace their current QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024 software. The scope includes core ERP modules (accounts payable/receivable, general ledger, budgeting, project accounting, purchasing, fixed assets, and cash management) along with implementation services, training, and ongoing support. The system will serve as the Authority's core financial system of record as it transitions from planning to final design and construction of a 1.5 million acre-foot reservoir project in California.
data.periodOfPerformance: Base year of 1 year, with up to two optional 5-year extensions (maximum 11 years total)
data.title: Financial Enterprise Resource Planning Software and Services
